subject: fix(sancio): route from req.url, not req.query, to survive Vercel param-key renames
body:
@vercel/node silently renamed the catch-all key from `slug` to `...slug`
(literal three-dot prefix from the [...slug].ts filename), making
req.query.slug undefined for every Sancio request. Production logs
showed `url: '/api/sancio/projects?...slug=projects'` with
`rawSlug: undefined` — the value lived at req.query["...slug"]. Every
sancio endpoint 404ed across all consuming frontends; CI was green
because the dispatcher tests drove req.query.slug directly.

Switch routing to read req.url: strip the /api/sancio prefix, split the
remainder on /, drop empties. req.url is HTTP-level state Vercel cannot
rename out from under us. Drop the rawSlug field from the fallthrough
log; add queryKeys (key names only, not values, to avoid leaking auth
tokens or PII) so a future framework rename is immediately visible in
Vercel logs.

Rewrite dispatcher tests to drive req.url shaped like production
(including the `...slug=` rewrite query string) instead of synthetic
req.query.slug. Add a regression-guard test asserting that
req.query.slug alone does NOT dispatch — pinning the new contract so a
future "fallback to query" change surfaces here.

Update docs/AI_MISTAKES.md and CLAUDE.md with the lesson: for Vercel
catch-all routes, route from req.url, not req.query.

Tags: bugfix, sancio, vercel, dispatcher, testing, documentation
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 5
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: sancio-api-stability
Semver: patch
